title: The Emperor's Court

"'''The Emperor's Court'''" is a non-[[canon]]ical comic book from ''[[Star Wars Tales 14]]''.

==Plot summary==
[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] is in court for killing [[Greedo/Legends|Greedo]]. The plaintiff is Greedo's mother, [[Neela]]. After a few [[Alien/Legends|alien]] witnesses are called, Judge [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] declares their testimonies have no effect given that they are nonhuman. Han shows a disk of Greedo shooting first and missing, but it was obviously faked (a reference to the re-release when Greedo shot first). Han is sentenced to life imprisonment frozen in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] and is sent to [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]].

==Development==
The segment is a parody of ''[[Wikipedia:The People's Court|The People's Court]]''.

This spoof plays on several of the changes in the 1997 [[The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition|Special Edition]] of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']], primarily on the subject of [[Greedo/Legends|Greedo]] firing first at [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] instead of [[Han shot first|Han getting off the only shot]]. Another allusion to the Special Edition is Han protesting [[Lak Sivrak/Legends|Lak Sivrak's]] story by saying that Sivrak was not even present at the time, as he was replaced in the Special Edition by [[Ketwol/Legends|Ketwol]].

The [[8D smelter droid]] [[LU-L-N]] is a parody of reporter Doug Llewelyn, who reported on the television series ''The People's Court''.

Han Solo's continual rewinding of the [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] of Greedo firing at him and repeating of the line "back and to the right" is a reference to the Oliver Stone film [[Wikipedia:JFK (film)|''JFK'']].

[[W:c:muppet:Super Grover|Super Grover]] of [[W:c:muppet:Sesame Street|''Sesame Street'']] fame, [[W:c:muppet:Kermit the Frog|Kermit the Frog]] of the [[W:c:Muppets|Muppets]], [[Wikipedia:Robby the Robot|Robby the Robot]] from the film [[Wikipedia:Forbidden Planet|''Forbidden Planet'']], Stripe from the film [[Wikipedia:Gremlins|''Gremlins'']], Waldo from [[Wikipedia:Where's Waldo?|''Where's Waldo?'']], a [[Dulok/Legends|Dulok]], and [[Wikipedia:Barney Gumble|Barney Gumble]] of [[Wikipedia:The Simpsons|''The Simpsons'']] fame can each be seen among the jurors.
